name: Pectin
description: Pectin is a heteropolysaccharide and a rich source of galacturonic acid with applications in foods, pharmaceuticals, and a number of other industries. In the food industry, pectin is used in jams, jellies, frozen foods, and more recently in low-calorie foods as a fat and/or sugar replacer. Pectin is used to lower blood cholesterol and triglyceride levels, as well as the treatment of diabetes and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). It is also available in oral forumations and lozenges to treat raw or ulcerated mouth and throat sores.
